item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it's written and how you describe Foundation's interaction between this GOI. There just isn't an SCP in this article. If you added an SCP that the Foundation and the GOI intially conflicted over, then this would definitely get my upvote. But right now I really can't with there being no actual SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this, and adding that you could improve this immensely by ditching the interview and heavily abbreviating the Munich agreement. Seriously, [W I Z A R D C O P S] is a legit cool concept, and focusing on the bureaucracy involved is the easiest way to squander that cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a very nice building though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Picture description: Munich

Picture: serbian license plate, "University Niš" on the building, serbian flag on the building…

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
As already asked, would I like to know how to handle foreign groups in EN?
There are some SCPs that use SAPPHIRE or a russian GOI before this contest started, and they had no tag, so are all no-EN groups untagged?
I couldn't find something in the tag guide.

Thank you for any answer.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Pulled the tag mages-academy since it technically isn't an existing tag on the -EN wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have already the train. ^^
And yes, the name is "9¾" …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I like this, even if it's not extremely related to the GOIs. I have to admit a biased leaning for the occult, though, to be honest. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see it the same way:
Nice SCP, but where is Mages Academy in the whole part?
Anyway, I will use this "Sub-Group" for a Mages Academia-Tale and it a nice expansion of the GOI-canon ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As mentioned in chat, it's written well, an interesting idea, but just doesn't sufficiently explore the group, its characters, or consequences. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't warrant a Hub imo, because it's a sub-GoI of the Mages Academy, which already has a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A very nicely and comprehensively written new group of interest. I really liked how you explained how the Foundation interact with the GoI and the final portion from the DEA.

My only criticism of this article is that I do not think that the GoI itself warrants SCP designation. Certainly thaumaturgical artifacts and individuals employed by the GoI may warrant such designation if they posses extra normal abilities or properties, but the agency itself is not really anomalous (or is it?)

Something about that just doesn't sit right with me, I find myself asking why groups such as AWCY or Serpents Hand dont have their own SCP designation if this is the train of thought we are following.

None the less, a superbly written introduction into what could be a very interesting GoI. There arent many with whom the Foundation actively work with so further tales could be very interesting. Maybe this would be better suited to its own GoI Hub though, instead of a SCiP?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely Done Proasek :D
The warrents 10 points to Thauiel

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Omg, didn't expect you to post it so soon. You madman. Take ur upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which is funny, because the Mages Academy already has a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This page is now available in german.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah - although saying 'this isn't an SCP' can come off as narrow-minded, I think it's appropriate in this case. Nothing about this is paranormal, except for a fleeting reference to the group's members having magic objects or 'thaumaturgy', where the former should have their own SCP designations and the latter is close to meaningless. This is basically a GoI hub page, with no relevant GoI articles, dressed up in SCP article format for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Next time on Wizard Cops: Totally Not Azkaban!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Widerstand is a flat, boring character, and the Munich Agreement is a virtual carbon copy of the far-too-aptly-named Boring Agreement from Wilson's Wildlife Solutions. This is a setup article, plain and simple, with setup that is wholly lacking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I the only one who got a Harry Potter vibe from this?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how this reads exactly as it should. The document felt (in context) very real. Also nice how you included Mutti Merkel only for her to get amnesticized, haha :D.
+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The International GoI Contest is all I needed to bring out my inner weryllium, it seems.

Thanks to my team, "Ten Points to Thaumiel," my critters, and the various people who sorted me right out when I completely shat myself as I was uploading this. Oh, and also huge thanks to Grom, the author of the Mage's Academy GoI.
